groundingDensity: light-to-moderate and deliberately surgical — this chapter was already strongly grounded, so the pass concentrated on world-bound substitutions, material texture, and institutional/geographic precision while protecting the duel rhythm and the Sylvanas-approach beat structure

scenes.title: Sanctum and threshold
focus: Caer Darrow crypt-spine placement and Scholomance lower-hall texture
Antonidas / Violet Citadel notation continuity inside Kel'Thuzad's room
material specificity for desk, slate, retorts, preserving salts, grave-fungi, and specimen jars
Jandice Barov threshold handoff sharpened without expanding the beat

scenes.title: Mechanism, ethics, and scale
focus: Andorhal / Eastweald / Lordaeron geographic anchoring inside Jaina's reconstruction of the plague
Council of Six, Kirin Tor, and Violet Citadel framing for the necromancy ban and shared training
tactile support through chalk dust, flagstone, vellum, Dalaran blue, ward lines, and copied pages
argument rhythm preserved through the mechanism -> ethics -> mirror -> patronage reveal chain

scenes.title: Sylvanas's approach
focus: crypt-spine threshold responses and Barov stone under the descent
aftermath tableau sharpened through chair, chalked hands, and Kel'Thuzad at center
Sylvanas arrival grounded through cadence, leather boots, and ward-pulses without fragment expansion

constraintsObeyed.antiBloat: The Sylvanas-approach sequence stayed fragmentary and pressure-driven; no explanatory expansion was added to the final pulses or reveal.
constraintsObeyed.antiWiki: No lore paragraphs were inserted. Institutional and cosmological names surface only where Jaina and Kel'Thuzad would naturally invoke them in argument.
constraintsObeyed.pov: All grounding remains filtered through Jaina's arcane, field-trained, institutionally literate lens.
constraintsObeyed.subtext: Kel'Thuzad's appetite and Jaina's missing Sylvanas-correction remain implicit in beats and phrasing rather than explanation.

auditResults.overGrounding: none detected — additions stayed on scene-engine nouns and contact moments
